mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month tightens discount rates and financial conditions, which should depress S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is directional only when the policy rate is trending higher, and it fails if rate hikes coincide with stronger-than-expected growth or if equities already priced the move.
evidence_refs: fact:policy rate at 2.0% as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of months when FEDFUNDS is rising on a 3-month basis, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays above 0.03 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in UNRATE above its recent level indicates labor-market deterioration and falling household income growth, which should reduce near-term equity returns through weaker demand and lower earnings expectations over the next 20 trading days. The effect is activated by a meaningful unemployment jump rather than the level alone.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show positive 20d SPX returns in more than half of cases, or if the signal loses sign consistency across any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is rising while UNRATE is falling, labor-income growth and consumer demand improve together, supporting retail sales and earnings revisions that lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both labor indicators to move in the same expansionary direction.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS near 137473k and UNRATE near 6.1% as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS/declining UNRATE configurations do not outperform baseline 60d returns, or if either series alone explains the effect without the joint condition.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in INDPRO relative to recent months signals industrial slowdown and weaker cyclical earnings, which should pressure the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days as investors revise growth expectations downward. The signal is strongest when production momentum turns negative after a prior plateau.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect disappears after controlling for contemporaneous FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: An upward turn in RSAFS indicates improving nominal consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should support equities over the next 20 trading days by lifting sales expectations and operating leverage estimates. The signal fails if sales growth is only inflation-driven and does not translate into higher real activity.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises faster than CPIAUCSL on a 3-month basis
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 381169 and CPIAUCSL at 219.181 as of t-1mo/t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if sales acceleration above inflation does not correspond to higher 20d SPX returns, or if the rank relationship is unstable and flips sign across consecutive 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation trend, real policy rates stay accommodative and risk assets should outperform over the next 60 trading days because financing conditions remain easy. The mechanism is a relative-price story, not a pure inflation story, and it fails when inflation jumps force an imminent policy response.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by > 1.0pp
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.0% and CPIAUCSL at 219.181 as of the snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if periods with inflation running above the policy rate do not show positive 60d SPX excess returns, or if tightening expectations immediately invert the sign of the effect.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE signals a worsening labor market that should weaken equity returns over the next 20 trading days through lower household spending and higher recession probability. The pattern must be joint: employment loss alone is not sufficient unless unemployment also rises.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises over the same 2-month window
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.1% and PAYEMS at 137473.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration condition does not predict negative 20d returns better than either series individually, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 40% over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST is recovering after weakness, it signals improving housing demand and related spending, which should support the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days through a broad cyclical-growth impulse. The signal is short-horizon and should fail if housing starts improve only after policy-driven refinancing effects without broader demand confirmation.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises on a 2-month moving average basis
failure_condition: Reject if rising HOUST is not followed by positive 5d SPX returns more often than the unconditional baseline, or if the effect vanishes once labor indicators are included.
